reasoning_model_1: The audio content accurately matches the script 'You're back? Why didn't you tell me!' with no errors. The speech is highly fluent and natural with high audio fidelity. Regarding the required emotion 'Surprise': At 00:00, the phrase 'You're back?' is delivered with a distinct upward pitch inflection and increased intensity, perfectly capturing a sudden, pleasant surprise. At 00:01, the follow-up 'Why didn't you tell me!' maintains this high energy and faster pacing, consistent with an excited reaction. The emotional expression is vivid, clear, and matches the punctuation cues (question mark and exclamation point) effectively.
reasoning_model_2: The audio accurately reproduces the text content. The fluency is generally good, though the voice quality sounds slightly more muffled or lower fidelity compared to a high-definition recording. Regarding the emotion 'Surprise': The model adopts a breathless, hushed tone (00:00 'You're back'), which conveys a sense of shock or disbelief. The delivery of 'Why didn't you tell me' (00:01) continues this breathless style. While this effectively communicates a form of surprise (specifically shock or being stunned), the audio quality slightly dampens the vividness of the expression.
model_comparison: Both models successfully convey the emotion of surprise, but they take different stylistic approaches. Model 1 delivers a bright, high-energy surprise with sharp pitch changes that clearly signal excitement and questioning. This aligns very well with the standard interpretation of the text and punctuation. Model 2 opts for a breathless, shocked delivery, which is also a valid interpretation of surprise but feels slightly more dramatic and less 'everyday natural'. Additionally, Model 1 has superior audio fidelity (clarity and crispness), whereas Model 2 sounds slightly muffled. Model 1 is judged as superior due to the combination of crystal-clear audio and a very precise, standard prosodic contour for the requested emotion.
